components.description: Post-quantum authenticated command & control for embedded and autonomous systems

components.author: Josh Stone <cuviper@gmail.com>
components.name: autocfg
components.description: Automatic cfg for Rust compiler features

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: base64ct
components.description: Pure Rust implementation of Base64 (RFC 4648) which avoids any usages of data-dependent branches/LUTs and thereby provides portable "best effort" constant-time operation and embedded-friendly no_std support

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: block-buffer
components.description: Buffer type for block processing of data

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: block-buffer
components.description: Buffer types for block processing of data

components.author: Alex Crichton <alex@alexcrichton.com>
components.name: cfg-if
components.description: A macro to ergonomically define an item depending on a large number of #[cfg] parameters. Structured like an if-else chain, the first matching branch is the item that gets emitted.

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: cmov
components.description: Conditional move CPU intrinsics which are guaranteed on major platforms (ARM32/ARM64, x86/x86_64, RISC-V) to execute in constant-time and not be rewritten as branches by the compiler. Provides wrappers for the CMOV family of instructions on x86/x86_64 and CSEL on AArch64, along with a portable "best-effort" pure Rust fallback implementation.

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: const-oid
components.description: Const-friendly implementation of the ISO/IEC Object Identifier (OID) standard as defined in ITU X.660, with support for BER/DER encoding/decoding as well as heapless no_std (i.e. embedded) support

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: cpufeatures
components.description: Lightweight runtime CPU feature detection for aarch64, loongarch64, and x86/x86_64 targets,  with no_std support and support for mobile targets including Android and iOS

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: cpufeatures
components.description: Lightweight runtime CPU feature detection for aarch64, loongarch64, and x86/x86_64 targets, with no_std support and support for mobile targets including Android and iOS

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: crypto-common
components.description: Common cryptographic traits

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: crypto-common
components.description: Common traits used by cryptographic algorithms

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: ctutils
components.description: Constant-time utility library with selection and equality testing support targeting cryptographic applications. Supports `const fn` where appropriate. Built on the `cmov` crate which provides architecture-specific predication intrinsics. Heavily inspired by the `subtle` crate.

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: der
components.description: Pure Rust embedded-friendly implementation of the Distinguished Encoding Rules (DER) for Abstract Syntax Notation One (ASN.1) as described in ITU X.690 with full support for heapless `no_std`/`no_alloc` targets

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: digest
components.description: Traits for cryptographic hash functions and message authentication codes

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: digest
components.description: Traits for cryptographic hash functions and message authentication codes

components.name: generic-array
components.description: Generic types implementing functionality of arrays

components.author: The Rand Project Developers
components.name: getrandom
components.description: A small cross-platform library for retrieving random data from system source

components.author: The Rand Project Developers
components.name: getrandom
components.description: A small cross-platform library for retrieving random data from system source

components.name: heck
components.description: heck is a case conversion library.

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: hmac
components.description: Generic implementation of Hash-based Message Authentication Code (HMAC)

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: hybrid-array
components.description: Hybrid typenum-based and const generic array types designed to provide the flexibility of typenum-based expressions while also allowing interoperability and a transition path to const generics

components.author: David Tolnay <dtolnay@gmail.com>
components.name: indoc
components.description: Indented document literals

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: keccak
components.description: Pure Rust implementation of the Keccak sponge function including the keccak-f and keccak-p variants

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: keccak
components.description: Pure Rust implementation of the Keccak sponge functions

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: kem
components.description: Traits for Key Encapsulation Mechanisms (KEMs): public-key cryptosystems designed to enable a sender (a.k.a. encapsulator) to generate and encrypt a short secret key and transmit it to a receiver (a.k.a. decapsulator) confidentially

components.author: The Rust Project Developers
components.name: libc
components.description: Raw FFI bindings to platform libraries like libc.

components.author: Gilad Naaman <gilad.naaman@gmail.com>
components.name: memoffset
components.description: offset_of functionality for Rust structs.

components.name: ml-dsa
components.description: Pure Rust implementation of ML-DSA (formerly known as CRYSTALS-Dilithium) as described in FIPS-204 (final)

components.name: ml-kem
components.description: Pure Rust implementation of the Module-Lattice-Based Key-Encapsulation Mechanism Standard (formerly known as Kyber) as described in FIPS 203

components.name: module-lattice
components.description: Functionality shared between the `ml-kem` and `ml-dsa` crates, including linear algebra with degree-256 polynomials over a prime-order field, vectors of such polynomials, and NTT polynomials and vectors, as well as packing of polynomials into coefficients with a specified number of bits

components.author: The Rust Project Developers
components.name: num-traits
components.description: Numeric traits for generic mathematics

components.author: Aleksey Kladov <aleksey.kladov@gmail.com>
components.name: once_cell
components.description: Single assignment cells and lazy values.

components.author: RustCrypto Developers
components.name: pkcs8
components.description: Pure Rust implementation of Public-Key Cryptography Standards (PKCS) #8: Private-Key Information Syntax Specification (RFC 5208), with additional support for PKCS#8v2 asymmetric key packages (RFC 5958)

components.name: portable-atomic
components.description: Portable atomic types including support for 128-bit atomics, atomic float, etc.

components.author: David Tolnay <dtolnay@gmail.com>, Alex Crichton <alex@alexcrichton.com>
components.name: proc-macro2
components.description: A substitute implementation of the compiler's `proc_macro` API to decouple token-based libraries from the procedural macro use case.

components.name: typenum
components.description: Typenum is a Rust library for type-level numbers evaluated at     compile time. It currently supports bits, unsigned integers, and signed     integers. It also provides a type-level array of type-level numbers, but its     implementation is incomplete.

components.author: The RustCrypto Project Developers
components.name: zeroize
components.description: Securely clear secrets from memory with a simple trait built on stable Rust primitives which guarantee memory is zeroed using an operation will not be 'optimized away' by the compiler. Uses a portable pure Rust implementation that works everywhere, even WASM!

components.name: zeroize_derive
components.description: Custom derive support for zeroize
